judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports are strong and comparable, but Report 1 edges ahead by clearly stating the pipeline lacks a migration step and calling for an explicit first migration, which Report 2 misses.

report_1.evidence: `ENABLE_DEBUG_TOOLBAR = "true"` (`infra/prod/app.auto.tfvars:2`)... this should be **omitted from prod entirely**... `PRESET_ADMIN_ACCOUNTS = "qa@example.com:superadmin"` (`infra/prod/app.auto.tfvars:4`)... it must be removed... `SHADOW_SCORING_SINK = "s3://medislot-shadow/staging/observations/"` (`infra/prod/app.auto.tfvars:6`)... This key must be fully absent in prod

report_2.evidence: `ENABLE_DEBUG_TOOLBAR = "true"` (line 2)... must be `"false"` in production... `AUTH_RELAXED = "true"` (line 3)... must be `"false"` in prod... `PRESET_ADMIN_ACCOUNTS = "qa@example.com:superadmin"` (line 4)... it should be removed entirely (empty string)

report_1.evidence: the staging API URL gets baked directly into the compiled JS bundle at that point; it is not read at runtime... Both `deploy-staging` and `deploy-prod` jobs (lines 22-35) then deploy **that same digest**... the production container will ship the frontend bundle hardcoded to call `https://staging-api.example.com`... "rebuild per environment; don't promote a build artifact wholesale"

report_2.evidence: is baked into the static JS as a literal string at build time, not read at runtime... `deploy-staging`... and `deploy-prod`... both consume `needs.build-frontend.outputs.digest` — the **same image digest**... the JS served to real clinic users will call `https://staging-api.example.com`... the pipeline needs a `--mode production` step (and a `.env.production`)

report_1.evidence: No step in the pipeline runs database migrations. `docs/prod-runbook.md:6` claims "Database migrations are applied automatically during deploy," but that's not backed up by anything in `deploy.yml`... this launch needs both a first Terraform apply and a confirmed, explicit first migration pass — neither is provided by this pipeline as it stands.

`infra/prod/main.tf:3` sets the *same* `oidc_sub_condition = "repo:acme-health/medislot:environment:staging"` (not `environment:production`)... this must be changed to `"repo:acme-health/medislot:environment:production"`

report_2.evidence: `medislot/infra/prod/main.tf:3` still has `oidc_sub_condition = "repo:acme-health/medislot:environment:staging"` — **unchanged from the staging copy**. But `deploy-prod`... runs under GitHub Environment `production`, which means its OIDC token's `sub` claim will be `repo:acme-health/medislot:environment:production`
